title: torch-npu virtual environment requires CPU-base torch and +cpu variant torchvision
problem_description: When creating a clean virtual environment for torch-npu, `pip install torch-npu` resolves `torch` to the latest version (e.g., 2.11.0) which is incompatible with the system torch-npu (2.5.1). Additionally, torch-npu==2.5.1 declares a strict dependency on `torch==2.5.1+cpu` (CPU-only build), and installing vanilla torchvision causes `torchvision::nms` dispatch key conflicts with the CPU-base torch. This is the most common blocker in CUDA-to-NPU migrations using venvs.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Probe system torch-npu version first (`python -c 'import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.__version__)'`). 2. Install matching CPU-base torch from PyTorch index: `pip install torch==<version>+cpu --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`. 3. Install torch-npu==<version> from domestic mirror. 4. Install CPU-variant torchvision: `pip install torchvision==<version>+cpu --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu`. 5. Verify with `torch.npu.is_available()`.
artifact_evidence: execution_journal.jsonl:line 4 (280s duration for Phase 2 — version conflict resolution consumed most time)

involved_code_files.role: Entry script — imports torch and torch_npu for device routing
Dependency declaration — lists torch, torch-npu, torchvision without version pins

reasoning: Every CUDA-to-NPU migration that uses virtual environments will encounter this exact issue. The torch-npu package pins torch to the +cpu variant, which is non-obvious and causes cascading dependency conflicts. The fix approach is deterministic and applies regardless of project size or model complexity. This is the highest-priority reusable experience from this run.
